plugin.name: SKDMoonlight
plugin.author: Eric
plugin.purpose: Finds the good moonlight room during SKD taunt phase - NEW, still needs live testing

trigger.match: ^.*Shimmering moonlight floats through the cracks in the ceiling to grace the floor\.$
^You shout ridiculous insults at the Sea King, whose face reddens with rage\.$
^From somewhere nearby, you hear (.*?) shout ridiculous insults at the Sea King\.|^(.*?) shouts ridiculous insults at the Sea King, whose face reddens with rage\.$
^The Sea King leaps away, clinging to the ceiling overhead\.$
^The Sea King drops back down to the floor and glances over the wreckage\.$
^His eyes begin to glow red, and you feel your soul start to pull away from your body\.$

muclient.script:
dofile(GetInfo(60) .. "aardwolf_colors.lua") require "gmcphelper"
-- cnote: echoes @-colour-coded text locally, in colour function cnote(s) AnsiNote(stylesToANSI(ColoursToStyles(s))) end
-- gtell: sends a colour-coded message to the group, no local echo function gtell(msg) SendNoEcho("gtell " .. msg) end
-- =========================================================== -- Moonlight locator (from Kanaye's SKDAssistant idea) -- During the taunt phase there are moonlit arena rooms: bad ones -- (shimmering line, then a RANDOM-chance "moonlight caresses your -- cheek" + pain on entry) and the good one (shimmering, never a -- caress). Standing detection: mark bad rooms off the caress line; -- once 2 bad rooms are known, the next moonlit room is good by -- elimination and is announced. The mlf hunt auto-walks and tests. -- ===========================================================
-- The 12 SKD arena rooms: compass label + adjacent tanking spots. -- GMCP room numbers arrive as strings, so keys are strings. local ml_rooms = { ["28379"] = { label = "NA", tank = {"N"} }, ["28380"] = { label = "N", tank = {"NA", "NW", "NE"} }, ["28381"] = { label = "NW", tank = {"N", "W"} }, ["28383"] = { label = "W", tank = {"NW", "SA", "SW"} }, ["28384"] = { label = "WA", tank = {"W"} }, ["28387"] = { label = "SW", tank = {"W", "S"} }, ["28389"] = { label = "S", tank = {"SA", "SW", "SE"} }, ["28390"] = { label = "SA", tank = {"S"} }, ["28388"] = { label = "SE", tank = {"S", "E"} }, ["28385"] = { label = "E", tank = {"EA", "SE", "NE"} }, ["28386"] = { label = "EA", tank = {"E"} }, ["28382"] = { label = "NE", tank = {"N", "E"} }, }
local label_to_room = {} for num, d in pairs(ml_rooms) do label_to_room[d.label] = num end
local ml_bad = {} -- set: room -> true for known bad-moonlight rooms local ml_bad_queue = {} -- same rooms in sighting order (max 2 kept) local ml_announced = nil -- room called as good this cycle, if any
-- A circuit of the arena: N, NE, E, EA, SE, S, SA, SW, W, WA, NW, NA local hunt_order = { "28380", "28382", "28385", "28386", "28388", "28389", "28390", "28387", "28383", "28384", "28381", "28379", } local GMCP_ID = "3e7dedbe37e44942dd46d264" local hunting = false local hunt_route = {} local hunt_index = 0 local hunt_laps = 0 -- completed circuits this hunt local HUNT_MAX_LAPS = 3 -- The caress is a RANDOM chance each entry (instant when it fires), so a -- single entry can never prove a room good - we bounce in/out and only -- call a room good after HUNT_BOUNCES clean entries with no caress. local HUNT_BOUNCES = 5 -- clean in/out entries to call a moonlit room good (adjustable: mlf bounces N) local HUNT_PEEK = 0.25 -- short window after each entry to catch the (instant) caress local HUNT_MOVE_TIMEOUT = 6 -- seconds before a stuck move aborts the hunt -- Navigation is strictly one move at a time: we only ever issue the -- next mapper goto once room.info confirms arrival at hunt_await. local hunt_await = nil -- room number we are waiting to arrive at local hunt_mode = nil -- "walk" | "bounce_out" | "bounce_in" local hunt_move_tok = 0 -- token so a stale watchdog can't fire local eval_room = nil -- room we've arrived in and are judging local eval_shimmer = false -- shimmer (moonlit) seen in eval_room local eval_caressed = false -- caress (bad moonlight) seen in eval_room local test_room = nil -- moonlit room currently being bounce-tested local test_partner = nil -- adjacent room we bounce out to local test_count = 0 -- clean entries so far this test local hunt_alas = 0 -- consecutive blocked-move messages
local function hunt_note(msg) cnote("@x099[@x045SKDMoonlight@x099]@w " .. msg) end
function hunt_abort(reason) if hunting then hunting = false eval_room = nil hunt_await = nil hunt_mode = nil test_room = nil hunt_note(reason .. " - hunt @x196aborted@w. Type @x048mlf@w to go again.") end end
function ml_reset(name, line, wildcards) ml_bad = {} ml_bad_queue = {} ml_announced = nil hunt_abort("Moonlight moved") end
local function ml_message(details) local t = details.tank local spots if #t == 1 then spots = "@x048" .. t[1] elseif #t == 2 then spots = "@x048" .. t[1] .. "@w or @x048" .. t[2] else spots = "@x048" .. table.concat(t, "@w, @x048", 1, #t - 1) .. "@w or @x048" .. t[#t] end return "@x099(@x045SKD@x099)@w @x048GOOD MOONLIGHT@w at @x045" .. details.label .. "@w! Tank the Sea King at " .. spots .. "@w" end
-- Caress line seen: this room is bad moonlight. Records the fact only - -- it NEVER drives navigation (the hunt state machine owns movement). -- While hunting it just raises eval_caressed for the room we're judging. function bad_ml_line(name, line, wildcards) local room_num = gmcp("room.info.num") if not room_num or not ml_rooms[room_num] then return end if hunting and room_num == eval_room then eval_caressed = true end if ml_announced == room_num then gtell("@x099(@x045SKD@x099)@w Scratch that - @x045" .. ml_rooms[room_num].label .. "@w is @x196BAD@w moonlight, keep hunting!") ml_announced = nil end if not ml_bad[room_num] then ml_bad[room_num] = true table.insert(ml_bad_queue, room_num) if #ml_bad_queue > 2 then ml_bad[table.remove(ml_bad_queue, 1)] = nil -- a bad moonlight moved; forget the oldest end end end
-- Shimmering line seen: once 2 bad rooms are known, any other room -- showing it is the good one by elimination - call it immediately. -- During a hunt, the hunt logic owns the decision instead. function good_ml_line(name, line, wildcards) if hunting then -- shimmer = this room is moonlit; note it for the room we're judging if eval_room and gmcp("room.info.num") == eval_room then eval_shimmer = true end return end if ml_announced or #ml_bad_queue < 2 then return end local room_num = gmcp("room.info.num") if not room_num or not ml_rooms[room_num] then return end if ml_bad[room_num] then return end ml_announced = room_num gtell(ml_message(ml_rooms[room_num])) end
function cmd_ml(name, line, wildcards) local room_num = gmcp("room.info.num") local details = room_num and ml_rooms[room_num] if not details then cnote("@x099[@x045SKDMoonlight@x099]@w Not in the SKD arena - stand in the good moonlight room and try @x048ml@w again.") return end SendNoEcho("gtell " .. ml_message(details)) end
-- =========================================================== -- Moonlight hunt: mlf walks the arena, and in each moonlit room it -- bounces in and out - the way a person does it - because the caress -- is a RANDOM chance per entry (instant when it fires). Every entry: -- * caress this entry -> BAD moonlight, move on to the next room -- * clean entry -> count it; after HUNT_BOUNCES clean entries -- with no caress, call it GOOD and stop here -- Once 2 bad rooms are known, the next moonlit room is GOOD by -- elimination (no bouncing). Non-moonlit rooms are skipped after a -- quick glance. Navigation is strictly one move at a time. Laps -- repeat (up to 3) if nothing found. -- ===========================================================
-- Issue exactly one move and arm a watchdog. No other movement command -- may be sent until room.info confirms arrival at this target. function hunt_go(room, mode) hunt_await = room hunt_mode = mode hunt_move_tok = hunt_move_tok + 1 local tok = hunt_move_tok DoAfterSpecial(HUNT_MOVE_TIMEOUT, "hunt_move_watchdog(" .. tok .. ")", 12) Execute("mapper goto " .. room) end
-- If a move never lands (mapper confused, blocked, etc.) abort cleanly -- instead of letting the hunt wander into walls. function hunt_move_watchdog(tok) if not hunting then return end if hunt_await ~= nil and tok == hunt_move_tok then hunt_abort("Navigation stalled (couldn't reach the next room)") end end
-- Move on to the next circuit room (ends any test in progress). function hunt_next() test_room = nil test_partner = nil test_count = 0 hunt_index = hunt_index + 1 if hunt_index > #hunt_route then -- circuit done without finding the good room - go around again hunt_laps = hunt_laps + 1 if hunt_laps >= HUNT_MAX_LAPS then hunting = false hunt_await = nil hunt_mode = nil hunt_note("Walked the arena " .. HUNT_MAX_LAPS .. " times without finding good moonlight - giving up. @x048mlf@w to retry.") return end hunt_note("Lap done, no good moonlight found yet - going around again.") hunt_index = 1 end hunt_go(hunt_route[hunt_index], "walk") end
-- Pick an adjacent tank room to bounce out to, preferring one not -- already known bad so we take less avoidable pain. local function pick_partner(room) local tanks = ml_rooms[room].tank for _, lbl in ipairs(tanks) do local r = label_to_room[lbl] if r and not ml_bad[r] then return r end end return label_to_room[tanks[1]] end
function cmd_mlhunt(name, line, wildcards) if hunting then hunt_note("Hunt already running - @x048mlf stop@w to abort.") return end local room_num = gmcp("room.info.num") if not room_num or not ml_rooms[room_num] then hunt_note("You need to be inside the SKD arena to start a moonlight hunt.") return end local start_pos = 1 for i, r in ipairs(hunt_order) do if r == room_num then start_pos = i break end end hunt_route = {} for i = 1, #hunt_order do table.insert(hunt_route, hunt_order[((start_pos - 1 + i) % #hunt_order) + 1]) end hunting = true hunt_index = 0 hunt_laps = 0 hunt_await = nil hunt_mode = nil eval_room = nil eval_shimmer = false eval_caressed = false test_room = nil test_partner = nil test_count = 0 hunt_alas = 0 hunt_note("Moonlight hunt started - walking the arena. @x048mlf stop@w to abort.") hunt_next() end
-- A blocked move during a hunt: re-path from where we actually are. -- Give up after a few in a row rather than pinball off walls. function hunt_alas_line(name, line, wildcards) if not hunting or not hunt_await then return end hunt_alas = hunt_alas + 1 if hunt_alas > 3 then hunt_abort("Navigation blocked (kept hitting walls)") return end Execute("mapper goto " .. hunt_await) end
function cmd_mlhunt_stop(name, line, wildcards) if hunting then hunting = false eval_room = nil hunt_await = nil hunt_mode = nil test_room = nil hunt_note("Moonlight hunt @x196aborted@w.") else hunt_note("No hunt running.") end end
-- Declare a room good and stop the hunt. `reason` explains how we knew. local function hunt_stop_good(room, reason) test_room = nil hunting = false hunt_await = nil hunt_mode = nil ml_announced = room gtell(ml_message(ml_rooms[room])) hunt_note(reason .. " - @x045" .. ml_rooms[room].label .. "@w is @x048good moonlight@w, stopping here.") end
-- HUNT_PEEK after each entry, judge what we saw. -- caress -> BAD, move on -- moonlit, walk-in -> if the 2 bad rooms are already known, this other -- moonlit room is GOOD by elimination (no bouncing); -- otherwise start bouncing it -- moonlit, bounced -> another clean entry; after HUNT_BOUNCES of them -- with no caress, call it GOOD function hunt_judge() if not hunting or not eval_room then return end if eval_caressed then local bad = eval_room hunt_note("@x045" .. ml_rooms[bad].label .. "@w caressed - @x196BAD@w, moving on.") hunt_next() return end if hunt_mode == "walk" then if not eval_shimmer then hunt_next() -- not moonlit, skip it return end if ml_bad[eval_room] then hunt_next() -- already known bad, no need to test again return end if #ml_bad_queue >= 2 then -- both bad moonlights are known; this other moonlit room is the good one hunt_stop_good(eval_room, "Both bad moonlights found") return end -- fewer than 2 bads known: bounce-test this moonlit room test_room = eval_room test_partner = pick_partner(eval_room) test_count = 1 hunt_note("@x045" .. ml_rooms[test_room].label .. "@w is moonlit - checking in/out...") else test_count = test_count + 1 end if test_count >= HUNT_BOUNCES then hunt_stop_good(test_room, "No caress in " .. HUNT_BOUNCES .. " entries") else hunt_go(test_partner, "bounce_out") end end
-- GMCP drives the hunt: room.info = arrival (only acted on when it -- matches the room we're waiting for), char.status = combat check. function OnPluginBroadcast(msg, id, name, text) if id ~= GMCP_ID then return end if not hunting then return end if text == "char.status" then if gmcp("char.status.state") == "8" then hunt_abort("You're being attacked") end return end if text ~= "room.info" then return end local room_num = gmcp("room.info.num") if room_num ~= hunt_await then return end -- passing through / not there yet hunt_await = nil hunt_alas = 0 if hunt_mode == "bounce_out" then hunt_go(test_room, "bounce_in") -- at the partner, straight back in return end -- walk or bounce_in arrival: judge this entry once the lines have printed eval_room = room_num eval_shimmer = false eval_caressed = false DoAfterSpecial(HUNT_PEEK, "hunt_judge()", 12) end
-- Bounce count is user-tunable and saved next to the plugin so it -- survives reinstalls (SetVariable would not). local BOUNCES_FILE = GetPluginInfo(GetPluginID(), 20) .. "SKDMoonlight_config.txt"
function save_bounces() local f = io.open(BOUNCES_FILE, "w") if f then f:write("bounces=" .. HUNT_BOUNCES .. "\n") f:close() end end
function load_bounces() local f = io.open(BOUNCES_FILE, "r") if f then for l in f:lines() do local n = l:match("^bounces=(%d+)") if n then HUNT_BOUNCES = tonumber(n) end end f:close() end end
function cmd_set_bounces(name, line, wildcards) local n = tonumber(wildcards[1]) if not n or n < 1 or n > 99 then cnote("@x099[@x045SKDMoonlight@x099]@w Bounce count must be @x0481-99@w. Currently @x048" .. HUNT_BOUNCES .. "@w.") return end HUNT_BOUNCES = n save_bounces() cnote("@x099[@x045SKDMoonlight@x099]@w Moonlight bounce count set to @x048" .. HUNT_BOUNCES .. "@w (entries with no caress before a room is called good).") end
function cmd_help(name, line, wildcards) cnote("") cnote("@x099===== @x045SKD Moonlight Finder@x099 =====@w") cnote("@wFinds the good moonlight room during the SKD taunt phase.") cnote("@wStanding still: once @x0482@w bad rooms are known it auto-gtells the good one when") cnote("@wyou're in it. Or use @x048mlf@w to auto-walk the arena and find it hands-free.") cnote("") cnote("@wCommands:@w") cnote(" @Wml@w gtell good moonlight from the room you're standing in") cnote(" @Wmlf@w auto-walk the arena; in each moonlit room bounce in/out -") cnote(" a caress = bad, move on. Once @x0482@w bad rooms are known the") cnote(" next moonlight is auto-flagged good; otherwise no caress in") cnote(" @x048" .. HUNT_BOUNCES .. "@w entries = good. Stops standing in the good room.") cnote(" @Wmlf stop@w abort a running hunt") cnote(" @Wmlf bounces <#>@w set the no-caress entry count (now @x048" .. HUNT_BOUNCES .. "@w)") cnote(" @Wmlf help@w this help") cnote("") cnote("@D(The hunt uses the Aardwolf mapper to move. It stops itself if a taunt lands,") cnote("@D the phase changes, or you get attacked.)@w") cnote("") end
-- =========================================================== -- Lifecycle -- =========================================================== function OnPluginInstall() load_bounces() cnote("@x099[@x045SKDMoonlight@x099]@w loaded. Type @x048mlf help@w for commands.") end
function OnPluginEnable() load_bounces() end
